name: Scheduled Data Sync
description: Runs on a schedule to retrieve records from a source system (SharePoint list) and sync them to a destination system (Dataverse table). Handles creates and updates, and logs the sync results.
key_features: Retrieve and iterate over source records
Upsert pattern (create or update) in destination
Sync logging with counts and errors

flow_steps.description: Runs every night at 2:00 AM UTC.
Gets all items modified in the last 24 hours using a filter query.

flow_steps.action: Apply to each - process each item
flow_steps.description: Loops over all retrieved items. Concurrency set to 10 for performance.

inner_actions.description: Check if the record already exists in Dataverse by external ID.

true_branch.description: Update the existing record with new values.

false_branch.action: Add a new row in Dataverse
false_branch.description: Create a new record in Dataverse.

inner_actions.description: On success, increment the success counter.
On failure, log the error details.

flow_steps.description: Sends a sync summary email with success/error counts.
flow_steps.subject: Data Sync Complete - @{formatDateTime(utcNow(), 'yyyy-MM-dd')}

expressions_used: formatDateTime() - Format the date for emails and logs
length() - Check if Dataverse row exists
notes: Concurrency on Apply to each improves throughput but may hit API throttling limits.
Adjust the filter query to match your sync window and requirements.
The Dataverse connector requires a Power Apps or Power Automate Premium license.
Consider adding a Scope around the sync logic to catch and handle unexpected errors.
For large datasets (10,000+ records), consider using pagination with the SharePoint connector.
